title: Four Regrettings And A Funeral

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer encounters some mishap or frustration
jokes.punchline: His signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase, perfect character integration and economy, but low originality since it's overused

jokes.text: S-So lonely.
jokes.setup: Character appears to be isolated or abandoned
jokes.punchline: The pathetic, stammered admission of loneliness
jokes.explanation: Simple pathos humor, economical but not particularly clever

jokes.text: No sex without a ring.
jokes.setup: Character states a moral/religious principle
jokes.punchline: The blunt, matter-of-fact delivery of a sexual rule
jokes.explanation: Direct and economical but fairly predictable religious comedy

jokes.text: What about us, man? Get your own couch.
jokes.setup: Characters are apparently fighting over couch space
jokes.punchline: The territorial dispute over furniture
jokes.explanation: Solid visual comedy setup with decent delivery

jokes.text: People called him the 'sixth Simpson.' I'm not sure why.
jokes.setup: Marge describes how integral Chip was to their family
jokes.punchline: Her oblivious confusion about why he was called the sixth Simpson
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven humor with Marge's typical obliviousness

jokes.text: He often spoke of that asterisk.
jokes.setup: Homer mentions Chip's wind-aided long jump record
jokes.punchline: The emotional weight Homer gives to an athletic technicality
jokes.explanation: Clever observation about athletic pride and technicalities

jokes.text: Your marriage is a dead fish floating in a tank of stale beer. Your baby's addicted to pacifiers.
jokes.setup: Lovejoy claims he has no regrets
jokes.punchline: His brutal assessment of Homer's life circumstances
jokes.explanation: Sharp, character-specific insults with vivid imagery. Lovejoy's cynicism perfectly deployed

jokes.text: Ow! My knees! 40 years of healing, gone!
jokes.setup: Lovejoy tells everyone to kneel
jokes.punchline: His immediate physical collapse and dramatic overreaction
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with appropriate escalation

jokes.text: He's such a little dickens. Although Dickens was pretty well behaved.
jokes.setup: Marge uses common expression about Bart being a 'little dickens'
jokes.punchline: Her literal interpretation comparing to Charles Dickens
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that's perfectly in character for Marge's literal-mindedness

jokes.text: Overstay my welcome for 40 years?
jokes.setup: Marge asks Homer to do what Gene Simmons does
jokes.punchline: Homer's critique of KISS's long career
jokes.explanation: Clever cultural commentary disguised as character confusion

jokes.text: It's a little-known fact that you can get head lice from peaches. Uh, this is my daughter Peaches.
jokes.setup: Man shares 'fact' about getting lice from peaches
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's talking about his daughter named Peaches
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with absurd payoff

jokes.text: We'll have more regrets at 11:00 tonight following an all-new bottle of scotch.
jokes.setup: Kent doing standard news sign-off
jokes.punchline: Replacing 'episode' with 'bottle of scotch'
jokes.explanation: Good news parody with character-appropriate alcoholism reference

jokes.text: Technology is a fad. Heavy, black balls are the future.
jokes.setup: Homer sells Apple stock to buy a bowling ball
jokes.punchline: His completely backwards assessment of future trends
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic with great dramatic irony given Apple's success

jokes.text: If it makes you feel worse, I'm the man who bought the stock you sold.
jokes.setup: Rich man tries to comfort Homer
jokes.punchline: Reveals he directly profited from Homer's mistake
jokes.explanation: Good dark twist on attempted consolation

jokes.text: I'm Father O'Greedy. Dimes for the needy.
jokes.setup: Priest introduces himself
jokes.punchline: Name that directly contradicts priestly values while rhyming
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character naming joke with good economy

jokes.text: My pants, like the Lord, work in mysterious ways.
jokes.setup: Priest's pants have fallen down
jokes.punchline: Comparing his wardrobe malfunction to divine will
jokes.explanation: Brilliant religious parody with perfect setup and economical delivery

jokes.text: Speaking of return on investments, my Apple stock is up 3,500%.
jokes.setup: Priest says 'return those vestments'
jokes.punchline: Pivots to boasting about stock returns
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier Apple stock joke with character-appropriate greed

jokes.text: Better go to the lumber yard. 'Lum-bar.' It means 'lower back.'
jokes.setup: Marge mentions lumbar support
jokes.punchline: Homer's confusion between lumber and lumbar
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer malapropism, very in-character but predictable

jokes.text: Burn, dumb, helpful books.
jokes.setup: Homer is frustrated with instructional material
jokes.punchline: His contradictory description of books as both dumb and helpful
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's anti-intellectual contradictions

jokes.text: I was referring to him.
jokes.setup: Burns appears to comment on Bart's youthful energy
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was actually talking about someone else falling
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Burns' characteristic callousness

jokes.text: There's heat. I just don't turn it on.
jokes.setup: Lilah mentions the unheated garret
jokes.punchline: Burns' casual admission of unnecessary cheapness
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Burns' lifelong miserliness

jokes.text: Wrong box. Lilah, will you take that big step from cousin to wife?
jokes.setup: Burns proposing to Lilah
jokes.punchline: Revealing they're cousins proposing marriage
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about aristocratic cousin marriage - fits Burns's old-money background

jokes.text: from cousin to wife?
jokes.setup: Burns proposes to Lilah romantically
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation they're cousins
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about aristocratic family dynamics, economically delivered

jokes.text: When you say 'others,' could they be bars of gold?
jokes.setup: Lilah asks Burns to think of others
jokes.punchline: His attempt to reinterpret altruism materialistically
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy showing Burns' inability to comprehend altruism

jokes.text: No one's that good.
jokes.setup: Lilah says she'll find someone as good as Burns is evil
jokes.punchline: Burns' matter-of-fact acknowledgment of his evilness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns line showing his casual self-awareness of his evil nature

jokes.text: Hemingway! Picasso. Matador Juan Belmonte. And Stalin. That man could really put pressure on you.
jokes.setup: Burns listing his old friends
jokes.punchline: Stalin described as applying pressure rather than mass murder
jokes.explanation: Dark understatement about Stalin - fits Burns's casual relationship with historical evil

jokes.text: That man could really put pressure on you.
jokes.setup: Burns mentions Stalin among his old friends
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic description of Stalin's murderous regime
jokes.explanation: Dark historical humor with perfect understatement

jokes.text: I was flying from DC to New York and, of course, I had the layover in Springfield.
jokes.setup: Maddow explains her presence at the funeral
jokes.punchline: Springfield being a logical layover between major cities
jokes.explanation: Mild geographical absurdity but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Kent, I'm gonna tell you the most important mistake you ever made... after this break. Just tell me already! After this break.
jokes.setup: Rachel teasing a big revelation
jokes.punchline: Using TV commercial break structure in personal conversation
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about TV conventions applied to real conversation - repeated for effect

jokes.text: you were suckling the dual teats of infotainment and celebrifawning.
jokes.setup: Maddow criticizes Kent's career choices
jokes.punchline: Vivid, crude metaphor for shallow journalism
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay with vivid imagery critiquing media culture

jokes.text: No! No, don't show it. There aren't five good seconds in the whole film.
jokes.setup: News show wants to show clip of actor's movie
jokes.punchline: Actor desperately trying to prevent showing his bad work
jokes.explanation: Good Hollywood satire about promotional obligations

jokes.text: I keep discovering a new rock bottom.
jokes.setup: Kent describes himself as a geologist
jokes.punchline: Metaphor for his career's continuous decline
jokes.explanation: Clever metaphor combining literal and figurative meaning

jokes.text: Mario's down the street found a rat in their pasta. When did that happen? Whenever you want.
jokes.setup: Tony suggests a news story about restaurant contamination
jokes.punchline: Implies he can plant evidence whenever needed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fat Tony moment showing criminal approach to news creation

jokes.text: At Mario's, the picture of Sinatra on the wall is signed by Frank Junior. The frame hides the 'Junior'!
jokes.setup: Rachel investigating Mario's restaurant
jokes.punchline: Uncovering a minor deception about the Sinatra photo
jokes.explanation: Mild investigative journalism parody - the revelation is appropriately mundane

jokes.text: The frame hides the 'Junior'!
jokes.setup: Investigation of Mario's restaurant
jokes.punchline: The petty deception about Frank Sinatra autograph
jokes.explanation: Minor investigative comedy but lacks punch

jokes.text: Willikers! In my three years of soft news, I've never seen anything like this.
jokes.setup: Kent discovering the tire dumping
jokes.punchline: Over-dramatic reaction to minor environmental violation
jokes.explanation: Kent's overreaction to mundane news fits his dramatic personality

jokes.text: It would explode if people gripped the wheel too tightly.
jokes.setup: Krusty explains his failed car venture
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously dangerous design flaw
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist product failure joke in Krusty's style

jokes.text: As the 4:00 p.m. clown, I have complete control of the Channel 6 news division.
jokes.setup: Krusty threatens to suppress the story
jokes.punchline: Absurd corporate hierarchy putting clown in charge of news
jokes.explanation: Excellent media satire about corporate priorities and power structures

jokes.text: People only want news at 6:00 p.m. from white guys on weekdays and black guys on weekends.
jokes.setup: Krusty dismisses cable news
jokes.punchline: Blunt observation about racial tokenism in broadcasting
jokes.explanation: Sharp media criticism but potentially problematic racial humor

jokes.text: Sorry. He 'isn't' coming.
jokes.setup: Tony insults Kent with poor grammar
jokes.punchline: His pedantic correction of his own grammar
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tony's unexpected attention to grammar

jokes.text: Oh, look, he's over Greenland!
jokes.setup: Maddow mocks local news Santa tracking
jokes.punchline: The mindless enthusiasm for tracking obvious movement
jokes.explanation: Predictable criticism of local news holiday coverage

jokes.text: She's still alive, and she just got over being mad at you last week.
jokes.setup: Burns learning about Lilah's status
jokes.punchline: The timing coincidence of her getting over anger
jokes.explanation: Convenient plot development played for mild humor - not particularly strong

jokes.text: Jesus? No, he's a real Eagle Scout, that one.
jokes.setup: Burns asks if Jesus beats Lilah
jokes.punchline: Casual endorsement of Jesus as a good guy
jokes.explanation: Mild religious humor with casual tone

jokes.text: Married to Jesus, eh? Does he beat her?
jokes.setup: Learning Lilah is married to Jesus as a nun
jokes.punchline: Burns asking if Jesus is abusive
jokes.explanation: Dark humor perfectly suited to Burns's evil character - casual blasphemy

jokes.text: Those raked rocks could have been me.
jokes.setup: Learning Lilah became a Buddhist nun with Zen garden
jokes.punchline: Burns' jealousy of inanimate rocks
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor showing Burns' narcissism and romantic delusion

jokes.text: Who moved my desk?!
jokes.setup: Burns threatens Charlie
jokes.punchline: Burns apparently made Charlie's desk disappear
jokes.explanation: Standard visual gag showing Burns' power but not particularly creative

jokes.text: Fox News. Is this really how I want to get back in the big leagues?
jokes.setup: Kent considering Fox News job
jokes.punchline: Questioning if Fox News counts as 'big leagues'
jokes.explanation: Mild political satire - predictable Fox News joke without much creativity

jokes.text: Sir, we are The New York Times.
jokes.setup: Kent asks if they sell the New York Times
jokes.punchline: The newspaper itself confirming their dire straits
jokes.explanation: Good commentary on print media's decline

jokes.text: 50% attention-seeking, 50% to drop things on people.
jokes.setup: Asked why he's causing trouble
jokes.punchline: Bart's honest, clinical self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's self-awareness

jokes.text: At the time, I didn't know it was illegal to sex up a cow on the Capitol steps.
jokes.setup: Example of Republican scandal Fox would cover up
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously specific and bizarre criminal act
jokes.explanation: Absurdly specific scandal but pushes boundaries of taste

jokes.text: At the time, I didn't know it was illegal to sex up a cow on the Capitol steps. ...we simply do this.
jokes.setup: Fox News demonstration of damage control
jokes.punchline: Cutting away from scandalous Republican confession
jokes.explanation: Political satire about media manipulation - the visual component likely enhances this

jokes.text: I'm sorry, I don't see any 'hops petals' near you. Deleting all contact information.
jokes.setup: Burns asks Siri for nearby hospital
jokes.punchline: Siri's misinterpretation and destructive overreaction
jokes.explanation: Good technology satire about voice recognition failures

jokes.text: I'm sorry, I don't see any 'hops petals' near you. Deleting all contact information. Delete confirmed.
jokes.setup: Siri misunderstanding 'hospital'
jokes.punchline: Escalating to deleting all contacts
jokes.explanation: Satirizes AI assistant failures with escalating consequences - relatable frustration

jokes.text: Housekeeping? Send up a Chinese dressing screen and one of Firestone's finest vulcanized prophylactics.
jokes.setup: Burns prepares for romance with Lilah
jokes.punchline: His overly formal, clinical request for condom
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns vocabulary applied to intimate situation

jokes.text: With my pump.
jokes.setup: Burns deciding to do things the old-fashioned way
jokes.punchline: Implies he needs mechanical assistance for arousal
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Burns' age and frailty but borders on crude

jokes.text: This kid's corpse could be my magic carpet.
jokes.setup: Kent sees Bart in danger
jokes.punchline: His callous view of child's death as career opportunity
jokes.explanation: Dark media satire showing reporter's callous opportunism

jokes.text: Dear Lord, please help me land safely. Or transform this basket into a flying killer robot that I control with my mind. Amen.
jokes.setup: Bart praying for safety
jokes.punchline: Escalating to ridiculous robot transformation request
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart prayer - starts reasonable then becomes absurdly ambitious

jokes.text: Or transform this basket into a flying killer robot that I control with my mind. Amen.
jokes.setup: Bart praying for help while floating
jokes.punchline: His prayer devolving into violent fantasy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart moment showing his inability to be sincere even in crisis

jokes.text: That's not a prayer. My God says it is.
jokes.setup: Lisa criticizing Bart's prayer
jokes.punchline: Bart claiming his god accepts it
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic - Bart's flexible theology versus Lisa's principles

jokes.text: My God says it is.
jokes.setup: Criticized for his inappropriate prayer
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual assertion of personal deity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's rebellious theology

jokes.text: Sir, that's five minutes. You can stop thinking of other people now.
jokes.setup: Burns has been helping homeless person
jokes.punchline: Smithers treating altruism as a timed exercise
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Burns' limited understanding of altruism

jokes.text: You know what, I think I'll give it another five.
jokes.setup: Smithers says Burns can stop being altruistic
jokes.punchline: Burns deciding to extend his charity
jokes.explanation: Nice character growth moment with Burns showing unexpected development

jokes.text: You know what, I think I'll give it another five. I'm proud of you, Monty.
jokes.setup: Burns completing his required kindness time
jokes.punchline: Choosing to continue and earning approval
jokes.explanation: Character growth moment - Burns developing actual compassion

jokes.text: I ain't eatin' that.
jokes.setup: Burns offers soup to homeless man
jokes.punchline: Homeless man's rejection of charity
jokes.explanation: Weak punchline that doesn't add much beyond rejecting help

jokes.text: Pushing, always... always pushing.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Ralph's intelligence despite Mozart/Churchill prenatal exposure
jokes.punchline: Ralph's confused complaint about refrigerator opening
jokes.explanation: Classic Ralph non-sequitur showing his confusion about basic appliances

jokes.text: Think's it's his brother.
jokes.setup: Lou mentions Ralph won't flush toilet
jokes.punchline: Ralph's bizarre anthropomorphic interpretation
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ralph absurdity showing his strange relationship with everyday objects

jokes.text: which we changed to look like Eugene Levy.
jokes.setup: Cannonballs were melted for Joe Paterno statue
jokes.punchline: The bizarre solution to problematic statue
jokes.explanation: Good topical humor about scandal statue conversions

jokes.text: Because their terrific products keep track of it for you. And anything else you could ever think of.
jokes.setup: Someone asks why everyone knows Apple stock prices
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic praise of Apple's ubiquitous information tracking
jokes.explanation: Mild technology criticism but lacks punch

jokes.text: Apple closed up another 20 points today. Why does everyone know? Because their terrific products keep track of it for you.
jokes.setup: Stock report about Apple gains
jokes.punchline: The very Apple products tracking their success
jokes.explanation: Final twist on the Apple stock running gag - ironic but not particularly strong

jokes.text: Homer's rising faster than Apple stock.
jokes.setup: Homer is floating up with balloons
jokes.punchline: Comparison to the running Apple stock gag
jokes.explanation: Solid callback that ties together episode's themes

jokes.text: As long as your family is together, every choice you've ever made is the right one.
jokes.setup: Homer reflects on regrets
jokes.punchline: His simplistic but touching philosophy
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's emotional wisdom despite his flaws

jokes.text: between our weekend prison documentary block and the two hours it takes to make up Joe Scarborough on Monday morning.
jokes.setup: Maddow offers Kent a job at MSNBC
jokes.punchline: The absurd programming schedule and makeup requirements
jokes.explanation: Decent cable news satire but not particularly sharp

tmdb_overview: Kent Brockman has decided that local news has been a real mistake and a terrible career move for him.
